title: Harvester Deploys Linux GoGra Backdoor in South Asia Using Microsoft Graph API
summary: The threat actor known as Harvester has been attributed to a new Linux version of its GoGra backdoor deployed as part of attacks likely targeting entities in South Asia.
card_summary: The threat actor known as Harvester has been attributed to a new Linux version of its GoGra backdoor deployed as part of attacks likely targeting entities in South Asia.

sections.technical:
The threat actor known as Harvester has been attributed to a new Linux version of its GoGra backdoor deployed as part of attacks likely targeting entities in South Asia.
"The malware uses the legitimate Microsoft Graph API and Outlook mailboxes as a covert command-and-control (C2) channel, allowing it to bypass traditional perimeter network defenses," the Symantec and Carbon Black Threat Hunter
sections.impact:

"The malware uses the legitimate Microsoft Graph API and Outlook mailboxes as a covert command-and-control (C2) channel, allowing it to bypass traditional perimeter network defenses," the Symantec and Carbon Black Threat Hunter. Severity is rated high.

title: Lotus Wiper Malware Targets Venezuelan Energy Systems in Destructive Attack
summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented data wiper that has been used in attacks targeting Venezuela at the end of last year and the start of 2026.
card_summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented data wiper that has been used in attacks targeting Venezuela at the end of last year and the start of 2026.
email_summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented data wiper that has been used in attacks targeting Venezuela at the end of last year and the start of 2026. Dubbed Lotus Wiper, the novel file wiper has been used in a destructive campaign targeting the energy and utilities sector in Venezuela, per findings from Kaspersky.

title: Toxic Combinations: When Cross-App Permissions Stack into Risk
summary: On January 31, 2026, researchers disclosed that Moltbook, a social network built for AI agents, had left its database wide open, exposing 35,000 email addresses and 1.5 million agent API tokens across 770,000 active agents.
card_summary: On January 31, 2026, researchers disclosed that Moltbook, a social network built for AI agents, had left its database wide open, exposing 35,000 email addresses and 1.5 million agent API tokens across 770,000 active agents.
quick_take: On January 31, 2026, researchers disclosed that Moltbook, a social network built for AI agents, had left its database wide open, exposing 35,000 email addresses and 1.5 million agent API tokens across 770,000 active…
email_summary: On January 31, 2026, researchers disclosed that Moltbook, a social network built for AI agents, had left its database wide open, exposing 35,000 email addresses and 1.5 million agent API tokens across 770,000 active agents. The more worrying part sat inside the private messages.

title: Microsoft Patches Critical ASP.NET Core CVE-2026-40372 Privilege Escalation Bug
summary: Microsoft has released out-of-band updates to address a security vulnerability in ASP.NET Core that could allow an attacker to escalate privileges.
card_summary: Microsoft has released out-of-band updates to address a security vulnerability in ASP.NET Core that could allow an attacker to escalate privileges.
email_summary: Microsoft has released out-of-band updates to address a security vulnerability in ASP.NET Core that could allow an attacker to escalate privileges. Tracked as CVE-2026-40372. Severity is rated high.

title: Cohere AI Terrarium Sandbox Flaw Enables Root Code Execution, Container Escape
summary: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in a Python-based sandbox called Terrarium that could result in arbitrary code execution.
card_summary: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in a Python-based sandbox called Terrarium that could result in arbitrary code execution.

sections.technical:
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5752, is rated 9.3 on the CVSS scoring system.
"Sandbox escape vulnerability in Terrarium allows arbitrary code execution with root privileges on a host process via JavaScript prototype chain traversal," according to A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in a Python-based sandbox called Terrarium that could result in arbitrary code execution.

Referenced vulnerabilities: CVE-2026-5752.

email_summary: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in a Python-based sandbox called Terrarium that could result in arbitrary code execution. Tracked as CVE-2026-5752. Severity is rated critical.
